revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:انجيو جى]][[de:Engyō-ji]][[en:Engyō-ji]][[eu:Engyō-ji]][[fr:Engyō-ji]][[id:Kuil Engyō-ji]][[it:Engyō-ji]][[ja:圓教寺 (姫路市)]][[pl:Engyō-ji]][[zh:圓教寺 (姬路市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Engyoji05s4592.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|qid=Q855964|1=Tendai}}|prefecture={{ill|Himeji|qid=Q209801|1=Himeji}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.891139|N|134.658139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Shōkū|qid=Q11491623|1=Shōkū}}|deity={{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|qid=Q11446712|1=Cintāmaṇicakra}}|part_of={{ill|Q11498714|qid=Q11498714|1=Q11498714}}; {{ill|Q11498748|qid=Q11498748|1=33 Temple Pilgrimage of Harima and Saigoku}}; {{ill|Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage|qid=Q750721|1=Saigoku Kannon Pilgrimage}}; {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}}}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=September 2021}} {{more citations needed|date=October 2021}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Shoshazan Engyō-ji | native_name = | image = Engyoji05s4592.jpg | image_size = 300px | alt = | caption = One of the main buildings in the Shoshazan Engyoji Temple complex. | map_type = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_caption = | location = 2968, Shosha, Himeji, Hyōgo 671-2201 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.891139|134.658139|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:landmark_region:JP_scale:10000}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|qid=Q855964|lt=Tendai|1=Tendai}} | deity = Nyoirin Kannon (Cintamāṇicakra) | country = Japan | functional_status = | website = http://www.shosha.or.jp/ | founded_by = Shoku | year_completed = 966 }} The {{nihongo|'''Shoshazan Engyō-ji'''|書写山圓教寺 or less commonly 書寫山圓教寺}} is a temple of the {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|qid=Q855964|lt=Tendai|1=Tendai}} sect in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. == History == It was founded by [[Shoku Shonin]] in 966. The complex of buildings is at the top of Mt Shosha approximately 25 minutes by bus from Himeji Station. The mountain summit can be reached by either a one-mile hiking trail or [[:en:Mt. Shosha Ropeway|Mt. Shosha Ropeway]], and is often visited by pilgrims. Scenes from ''{{ill|The Last Samurai|en|The Last Samurai|qid=Q207130|lt=The Last Samurai|1=The Last Samurai}}'' were filmed there. Engyō-ji is temple No. 27 in the [[:en:Kansai Kannon Pilgrimage|Kansai Kannon Pilgrimage]], following {{ill|Ichijō-ji|en|Ichijō-ji|qid=Q3147650|lt=Ichijō-ji|1=Ichijō-ji}} and preceding [[Nariai-ji]]. == Building list == *Bentendō *[[Daikōdō]] - {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property of Japan|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. It was rebuilt in {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. *Daikokudō *Fudōdō *[[Gohōdō]] - Important Cultural Property of Japan. It was rebuilt in 1559. *Gohōdō haiden - It was rebuilt in 1589. *Gyōjadō *Hokkedō *[[Jikidō]] - Important Cultural Property of Japan. It was rebuilt in Muromachi period. *[[Jōgyōdō]] - Important Cultural Property of Japan. It was rebuilt in Muromachi period. *Jujiin *Jumyōin - Kyakuden, Kuri and Karamon is Important Cultural Property of Japan. *[[Juryōin]] - Important Cultural Property of Japan. *Kaizandō - Important Cultural Property of Japan. It was rebuilt in {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. *[[Kongodō]] - Important Cultural Property of Japan. It was rebuilt in Muromachi period. *Maniden - It was rebuilt in 1933. *Monjudō *Myōkōin *Sengakuin *[[Shōrō]] - Important Cultural Property of Japan. It was rebuilt in {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. *Yakushidō *Zuikōin <Gallery> File:Engyoji02st3200.jpg|Maniden File:Engyoji17s4592.jpg|Jikidō File:Engyoji34n4592.jpg|Shōrō File:Engyoji42nt3200.jpg|Jyōgyōdō File:Shosha Engyoji001.jpg File:Mt. Shosha cable car 01.jpg|Mount Shosha Ropeway </Gallery> == In media == Engyō-ji was one of the locations of the movie {{ill|The Last Samurai|en|The Last Samurai|qid=Q207130|lt=The Last Samurai|1=The Last Samurai}}. In the picture it is the temple of Lord Katsumoto.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Engyoji Temple – The Last Samurai’s Winter Hideaway |url=https://www.japan-guide.com/e/e3503.html |access-date=2022-09-30 |website=www.japan-guide.com/ |language=en}}</ref> It was also one of the filming locations of ''{{ill|Snake Eyes (2021 film)|en|Snake Eyes (2021 film)|qid=Q64027609|lt=Snake Eyes|1=Snake Eyes (2021 film)}}''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=IT |first=SCEEN |title=Snake Eyes: G.I. Joe Origins at Kishiwada Castle - filming location |url=https://www.sceen-it.com/sceen/5308/Snake-Eyes-G-I-Joe-Origins/Kishiwada-Castle |website=www.sceen-it.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Mukhopadhyay |first=Arka |date=22 July 2021 |title=Where Was Snake Eyes (2021) Filmed? |url=https://thecinemaholic.com/where-was-snake-eyes-2021-filmed/}}</ref> == References == <references /> == External links == {{Commons Category|Engyō-ji}} *[http://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/english/spot/67_engyoji.html Hyōgo Tourism - Engyoji] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170118104655/http://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/english/spot/67_engyoji.html |date=18 January 2017 }} *[http://www.shosha.or.jp/ Mt. Shosha - Engyoji] (Japanese) *[https://web.archive.org/web/20160121061746/http://www.himeji-kanko.jp/en/ Official Himeji tourism website] in English {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}
{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}
